The '''Yahk-Tosh''' were a slug-like [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Xolu]] in [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] and were believed to be distantly related to the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]]. Their bodies were slug like in appearance, with six thick [[Limb/Legends|arms]], which they used for moving around, and their scaled skin had a dirty green [[Color/Legends|color]]. Four thin horns sprouted from the tops of their heads and their pointed snouts were filled with rows of serrated [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]].

Like the Hutts, the Yahk-Tosh were also immune to [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]]. As well as their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] the species controlled the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Far Pando]] also in Hutt Space, but pledged their loyalty to the [[Grand Hutt Council/Legends|Hutt Grand Council]]. One Yahk-Tosh [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] named [[Gar-Oth]] [[Invasion of Lourdes' homeworld|subjugated]] [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Lourdes]]' [[Lourdes' homeworld|homeworld]], until he was killed by the Princess with the help of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Yoshi Raph-Elan]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Yahk-Tosh were a [[Slug/Legends|slug]] like [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] similar in appearance to the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] species<ref name="FF">{{FactFile|128|LOU 1-2|Princess Lourdes}}</ref> which they were believed to be distantly related to.<ref name="EA" /> Like the Hutts they had tough, [[Color/Legends|green]] hides, a single foot upon which they moved about as well as a similar facial structure consisting of two [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] containing a [[Tongue/Legends|tongue]], unlike the Hutts though they had six [[Limb/Legends|arms]] each ending in three digits,<ref name="SC" /> which they used to move about, scaly skin,<ref name="E3" /> [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] within their mouth<ref name="SC" /> and four horns growing from the top of their heads.<ref name="E3" /> The species had [[Color/Legends|red]] colored blood,<ref name="SC" /> but also produced slime<ref name="FF" /> and at least one member had yellow colored eyes.<ref name="SC" /> Also like Hutts<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> members of the species were able to resist [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]] by [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] beings and had some [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] abilities of their own which were strong enough to control some droids. The species were also surprisingly fast for their large size, capable of keeping up with a human, and they were also shockingly strong.<ref name="SC" />

==History==
The Yahk-Tosh lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Xolu]] within the region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] that became known as [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], which was controlled by the Hutt [[Species/Legends|species]] and lead by their [[Grand Hutt Council/Legends|Hutt Grand Council]], which the Yahk-Tosh pledged loyalty to. The region was first explored between [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]] and [[8000 BBY]] and Hutt space had expanded to include Xolu by the time of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] between [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]] and [[3996 BBY]]. Within Hutt space they also controlled the planet [[Far Pando]] which was a pleasant agrarian world. Both Xolu and Far Pando were on the [[Pando Spur]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] which connected them to [[Hollastin/Legends|Hollastin]] and [[Near Pando]], two other worlds within Hutt Space.<ref name="EA" />

One Yahk-Tosh [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] named [[Gar-Oth]] had come to control an [[Gar-Oth's Empire|empire]] by [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]] but in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]] he was [[Death/Legends|killed]] after successfully [[Invasion of Lourdes' homeworld|invading]] a small [[Lourdes' homeworld|planetoid]] with a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] army and attempting to marry it's ruler, [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Lourdes]], who stabbed him with the aid of a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Yoshi Raph-Elan]].<ref name="SC" /> Xolu and the Yahk-Tosh were still considered part of Hutt space by [[137 ABY]].<ref name="EA" />

==Yahk-Tosh in the galaxy==
One Yahk-Tosh, named Gar-Oth, became a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]<ref name="E1">{{CSWECite|I|324|Gar-Oth, Lord}}</ref> and controlled a small empire, which he ruled using an army of droids. Gar-Oth and his army invaded a small planetoid<ref name="SC" /> in a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] near to his empire,<ref name="FF" /> meaning to expand the empire, and when the local population resisted the droids killed many of them including the planetoid's [[Lourdes' father|king]]. Once his forces had control of the planetoid Gar-Oth decreed a number of laws<ref name="SC" /> which his [[GX1-series battle droid]]<ref name="GX1">''[[Scavenger's Guide to Droids]]''</ref> enforced on the local population. This included a curfew, which if citizens broke they would be punished with death, which the battle droids designated [[punishment code number 62719]]. Attempting to rescue a citizen from such punishment violated another decree Gar-Oth had made named [[decree code number 352719]] which was also punishable by death. Gar-Oth however craved legitimacy in his control of the planetoid and so arranged to [[Marriage/Legends|marry]] the daughter of the deceased king, Princess Lourdes. His droids continued to kill civilians until the princess agreed to his proposal. Whilst preparations for the marriage were made Lourdes was kept guarded by battle droids in a [[Lourdes' castle|castle]], which the Yahk-Tosh also had [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] monitor.<ref name="SC" />

Whilst Lourdes was held captive in the tower Gar-Oth grew rapidly angrier with the delay and when mystics he employed envisioned a [[The Foreseen|Foreseen]] who would attempt to stop the marriage he moved the date forward to that of his [[Gar-Oth's engagement ball|engagement ball]]. Upon arriving at the ball with a pair of [[Jilruan/Legends|Jilruan]] servants and his wedding gift to Lourdes', a droid named [[Goliath (droid)|Goliath]], he informed the princess that to avoid the Foreseen they would be married immediately. To ensure her acceptance he sent battle droids to a number of villages which were prepared to killed the inhabitants should Lourdes refuse. After Gar-Oth's insistence on marrying on the spot however, Lourdes servant declared that he was in fact her husband. In reality the [[Human/Legends|Human]] was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] named Yoshi Raph-Elan, whose [[Yoshi Raph-Elan's starfighter|starfighter]] had been shot down above the planetoid by droid starfighters belonging to the Yahk-Tosh. Enraged, Gar-Oth attacked Yoshi with his bare hands attempting to kill him as combat to the death between wedding suitors was legal by the planetoids royal law. Gar-Oth's droids were able to help him but none of his living servants as the law stated no living being could interfere. When the Jedi attempted unsuccessfully to use a mind trick on him, Gar-Oth realized that he was Force-sensitive and became even angrier, summoning Goliath, which he controlled telepathically, to kill Yoshi and Lourdes. Yoshi attacked the droid whilst Lourdes attacked her fiancé with a sword stolen from one of the Jilruans. Gar-Oth however overpowered Lourdes and began throttling her at which point a Jilruan offered to finish her off with his dagger but was stopped by a servant of Lourdes named [[Skeeter]]. Much to Gar-Oth's surprise Lourdes stabbed him with a second sword she had hidden beneath her robes and killed him, shortly after which Yoshi destroyed Goliath who toppled and crushed most of Gar-Oths attendants and droids. After the Yahk-Tosh's death Lourdes people reclaimed the planetoid from Gar-Oth's forces and Yoshi returned to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="SC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Yahk-Tosh first appeared in [[2001]] in the comic book [[Star Wars (1998) 27|''Star Wars'' (1998) 27]], an issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' series of comics produced by [[Dark Horse Comics]]. Written by [[Doug Petrie]], the comic featured Gar-Oth as its antagonist. While it did not identify Gar-Oth's species, it referred to the language he spoke as Yahk-Tosh. The species was first officially named as part of an entry on Princess Lourdes in [[De Agostini]]'s [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 128|128th]] issue of the ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' which was written by [[Andrew Darling]], [[Matthew Harvey]], [[Liz Holliday]] and [[Iain Lowson]] in [[2004]]. The species and Gar-Oth himself then both received an entry in the ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Stephen J. Sansweet]] in [[2008]] which established their possible relation to the Hutts. Their homeworld and control of Far Pando were both included in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' which was written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] in [[2009]] and published by [[Del Rey]]. Wallace also mentioned Gar-Oth in two blog posts he made concerning the Atlas where he identified the Yahk-Tosh's attendants as Jilruans, as it hadn't been directly stated in the atlas.<ref name="Blog-danwallace|144">{{Blog|danwallace|144|Endnotes for Star Wars: The Essential Atlas (part 2 of 5)}}</ref><ref name="Blog-danwallace|153">{{Blog|danwallace|153|Unstated Canon in Star Wars: The Essential Atlas}}</ref>
